---
title: "Analysis"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# Add the python directory to the path
# When running from quarto/, go up to Analysis/, then into python/
python_dir = Path('../python').resolve()
if str(python_dir) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(python_dir))

# Now import after path is set
from merge_data import merged_data
```

## Overview

This page contains statistical analysis of Chinese development projects.

## Regression Analysis: Public Opinion on Development Funds

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from regress import regress

# Aggregate development funds by country (in billions USD)
country_funds = merged_data.groupby('Country_of_Activity')['Amount_Constant_USD_2023'].sum().reset_index()
country_funds.columns = ['Country', 'Total_Funds']
country_funds['One Billion Dev-Dollars'] = country_funds['Total_Funds'] / 1e9

# Get average public opinion by country
country_opinion = merged_data.groupby('Country_of_Activity')['net_positive_adj'].mean().reset_index()
country_opinion.columns = ['Country', 'Net Positive Opinion']

# Merge funds and opinion data
regression_data = pd.merge(country_funds, country_opinion, on='Country', how='inner')

# Run regression with forest plot
results = regress(regression_data,
                  y_value='Net Positive Opinion',
                  x_value='One Billion Dev-Dollars',
                  label='Public Opinion on Development Funds')
```
